pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point to the mollusk species information site with quick access to search, browsing, and tools. The homepage surfaces a global search, shortcuts into advanced tools (Taxa, Traits, Map, Education), featured species/resources, and user tool summaries (collections, comparisons, favorites, bookmarks).
primary_functions: Provide a global search bar for species name or taxon queries that leads to the Species Search & Results page
Offer prominent links/buttons to Advanced Search, Taxa/Browse by Group, Traits search, Distribution Map, and Teaching Resources
Surface shortcuts to user tools such as Collections, Comparisons, Favorites, and Bookmarks
Introduce the site’s purpose and highlight featured species or teaching resources

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit global search form in the header or homepage hero
Click 'Advanced Search' button or header link
Click 'Taxa / Browse by Group' button or header link
Click 'Traits / Morphology' button or header link
Click 'Distribution Map' button or header link
Click 'Teaching Resources' button or header/footer link
Click a featured species card on the homepage
Click a featured teaching resource card on the homepage
Click 'Collections' shortcut or header link
Click 'Comparisons' shortcut or header link
Click 'Favorites' shortcut or header link
Click 'Bookmarks' shortcut or header link
Click 'About' link in footer
Click 'Help / How to Use' link in footer or header

access_methods.description: Main navigation entry via 'Home' link
Footer link 'Home'
Directly opened as site root or index.html

pages.name: Species Search & Results
pages.description: Unified page for simple search, advanced filters, trait-based search, and taxonomic browsing of mollusk species. Supports all discovery flows for species, including habitat, depth, size, region, conservation, trait filters, and sort controls.
primary_functions: Run keyword searches on species (e.g., typing 'tridacna' from the header search or homepage) and display matching species

Provide advanced filters including habitat type (marine, freshwater), depth range (minimum and maximum depth in meters), size filters (minimum shell/body length, minimum maximum length in cm), geographic region or country (e.g., North America, Indo-Pacific, Western Atlantic, Japan, Australia), status/impact (e.g., invasive/introduced), first record year (e.g., ≥ 1950), conservation status, and year described

Provide taxonomic filters to restrict results to specific ranks (e.g., family Muricidae, genus Tridacna) and support entry via a Taxa/Browse by Group mode (e.g., pre-filtering to cephalopods or Muricidae from the header)
Provide trait-based filters (Traits/Morphology mode) such as radula type (e.g., toxoglossan) and higher taxon restrictions (e.g., Gastropoda for snails)

Allow sorting of results by multiple criteria, including maximum recorded depth (highest first), maximum length (largest first), conservation status (most threatened first), and year described (oldest first)
Display a paginated list of matching species with key summary data (taxon, size, depth, region, status) and links to open each species detail page, including opening multiple species in new tabs

incoming_params.description: Keyword search term entered in the global search, e.g. 'Tridacna'
Search mode: 'basic', 'advanced', 'taxa', or 'traits'; controls which filter panels are emphasized
Taxon ID for a mollusk class (e.g., Cephalopoda) when entering via Browse by Group
Taxon ID for a family (e.g., Muricidae) when entering via Browse by Family
Generic taxon ID (e.g., genus Tridacna) used to restrict results via taxon filter
Preselected habitat type such as 'marine' or 'freshwater'
Preselected region filter (e.g., 'north_america', 'indo_pacific')
Preselected radula type for trait-based search (e.g., 'toxoglossan')
Active sort option, e.g., 'max_depth_desc', 'max_length_desc', 'conservation_status_severity_desc', 'year_described_asc'
Current results page number for pagination
Number of results per page; defaults from getSpeciesSearchFilterOptions.default_page_size

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a species scientific name in the results list
Click 'View on map' for a species in the results
Click 'Advanced filters' toggle within the page
Switch to 'Taxa / Browse by Group' tab within search
Switch to 'Traits / Morphology' tab within search

access_methods.description: 'Species Search' and 'Advanced Search' header links, and via 'Taxa / Browse by Group' or 'Traits / Morphology' links
Destination of global search form on the homepage with 'query' parameter
Directly accessible with appropriate query string parameters to restore a search context

pages.name: Species Detail
pages.description: Detail page for an individual mollusk species with taxonomy, traits, distribution, and user actions, including adding to collections, comparisons, and bookmarks.
primary_functions: Display complete species information including taxonomy, habitat (marine/freshwater), depth range, size (maximum length), geographic distribution (including regions like Japan, Australia, Western Atlantic), conservation status, invasive/introduced status, first record year, and morphological traits (e.g., radula type)

Allow the user to add the species to a collection or list: create a new collection/list (e.g., 'Deep Sea Study', 'Invasive NA Freshwater', 'Venomous snails', 'Giant Cephalopods') or select an existing one to add the species
Allow the user to add the species to a comparison: create a new comparison list (e.g., 'High-risk clams', 'Japan vs Australia') or select an existing comparison list and confirm the addition
Allow the user to bookmark the species for later reference and provide a link to open the Bookmarks page
Provide contextual links such as 'View on map' (to open Distribution Map pre-focused on this species) and links back to any collections or comparisons the species belongs to
Summarize key distribution information so that, when used within a comparison, distributions for different species can be meaningfully contrasted

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the species whose details should be displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View on map' in the distribution section
Click 'View collection' after adding to or selecting a collection
Click 'View comparison list' in the comparison panel
Click 'View bookmarks' after bookmarking a species

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'speciesId' query parameter from search results, map, collections, comparisons, or bookmarks
Species cards across the site link directly to this page

pages.name: Distribution Map
pages.description: Interactive distribution map for exploring species presence by region and generating region-based species lists. Supports multi-region intersection (e.g., Japan + Australia) and exclusion logic.
primary_functions: Display an interactive map with region and country filters allowing the user to select one or multiple regions (e.g., Japan and Australia simultaneously)
List species that match the current region filter, including the ability to highlight species present in all selected regions (e.g., species occurring in both Japan and Australia)
Allow the user to update region selections (e.g., remove Australia to show Japan-only species) and refresh the associated species list
Provide links from listed species to open their Species Detail pages (typically in new tabs) to then add them to comparisons or collections
Serve as the starting point for constructing region-based comparisons (such as 'Japan vs Australia') by enabling users to find species with overlapping or distinct distributions

incoming_params.description: Comma-separated region IDs to pre-select on the map (e.g., 'japan,australia')
Presence filter mode, e.g., 'present_in_all' or 'present_in_any' for selected regions
Comma-separated region IDs to explicitly exclude from results
When provided, the map centers and highlights this species' distribution

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a species name in the map's species list
Click 'View comparison' after adding species to a comparison like 'Japan vs Australia'

access_methods.description: Direct access via 'Distribution Map' header link
Accessed with optional parameters to preselect regions or highlight a specific species

pages.name: Teaching Resources
pages.description: Listing and search interface for educational and teaching materials related to mollusks. Supports keyword search, age-group filters, and publication year range selection for tasks like finding cephalopod camouflage resources for ages 12–14 from 2019–2024.
primary_functions: Allow users to search teaching resources via a keyword field (e.g., 'cephalopod camouflage') and display matching resources
Provide filters for target age or education level (e.g., ages 12–14 or middle school) and for publication year range (e.g., 2019–2024)
Display a results list of teaching resources that match the keyword and filters, including title, brief description, target age, and publication year
Link each resource in the list to its Teaching Resource Detail page where it can be favorited
Offer navigation back to the Home page and to other main sections via the header

incoming_params.description: Keyword query for teaching resources, e.g., 'cephalopod camouflage'
Preselected target_age_group value such as 'ages_12_14'
Lower bound of publication year filter (e.g., 2019)
Upper bound of publication year filter (e.g., 2024)
Current results page number for teaching resources pagination

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a teaching resource title in the results
Click 'My favorites' link or button

access_methods.description: 'Teaching Resources' header link
Footer link 'Teaching Resources'
Can be deep-linked with preset query and filters via URL parameters

pages.name: Teaching Resource Detail
pages.description: Detail view for an individual teaching resource with metadata and favorite controls, used for tasks like favoriting a cephalopod camouflage resource.
primary_functions: Show full information about the teaching resource, including title, description, target age range, publication year, authors, and any download or external links
Allow the user to add or remove the resource from their favorites via an 'Add to favorites' button or star icon
Provide a clear link or control to open the Favorites page to confirm that the resource has been saved
Allow navigation back to the Teaching Resources listing or to other main sections via the header

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the teaching resource to display

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'My favorites' or 'View favorites' after adding/removing a favorite
Click 'Back to results' or 'All teaching resources'

access_methods.description: Accessed from resource listings or favorites via 'resourceId' parameter

pages.name: Collections & Lists
pages.description: Management and viewing page for user-defined species collections and thematic lists. Supports reviewing and editing collections such as 'Deep Sea Study', 'Invasive NA Freshwater', 'Venomous snails', and 'Giant Cephalopods'.
primary_functions: Display a list of all existing collections/lists created from species detail pages (e.g., 'Deep Sea Study', 'Invasive NA Freshwater', 'Venomous snails', 'Giant Cephalopods') with species counts
When a collection is selected (via a parameter such as collectionId), show the species contained in that collection as a list with key summary info and links to each Species Detail page
Allow users to review and manage their collections by removing species from a collection or optionally renaming/deleting collections
Serve as the destination when users click 'View collection' or open 'My collections' from the header or from within species detail dialogs

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the collection to display; if omitted, the page shows an overview of all collections

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a species name within a selected collection
Click a collection name in the collections overview list

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Collections' header link
Deep-link to a specific collection using 'collectionId' query parameter

pages.name: Comparisons
pages.description: Page for viewing and managing species comparison lists with side-by-side data, including comparisons like 'High-risk clams' and 'Japan vs Australia'.
primary_functions: Display a list of existing comparison sets (e.g., 'High-risk clams', 'Japan vs Australia') created from species detail pages

When a comparison is selected (via a parameter such as comparisonId), present the included species side by side, highlighting key comparative fields such as size, depth range, conservation status, and especially distribution
Allow users to remove species from a comparison list or delete an entire comparison if desired
Serve as the target for 'View comparison list' links from species detail pages and from the comparison panel shown when adding species to comparisons

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the comparison set to display; if omitted, an overview of all comparison sets is shown

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a species name within a comparison table
Click a comparison set name in the comparisons overview list

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Comparisons' header link
Deep-link to a specific comparison using 'comparisonSetId' query parameter

pages.name: Favorites
pages.description: User’s list of favorited teaching resources for quick access. Supports review and removal of favorite resources such as cephalopod camouflage lessons.
primary_functions: List all teaching resources that the user has marked as favorite, showing title, age range, and publication year
Provide links from each favorite resource entry back to its Teaching Resource Detail page
Allow users to remove resources from their favorites directly from this listing
Provide navigation back to Teaching Resources and other main sections via the header

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a resource title in the favorites list
Click 'Browse all teaching resources'

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Favorites' header link
Linked from resource detail page after favoriting

pages.name: Bookmarks
pages.description: User’s list of bookmarked mollusk species. Used to confirm bookmarking actions such as saving the earliest described Western Atlantic Muricidae species.
primary_functions: List all species that the user has bookmarked, with key summary info (name, group, region or other key attributes)
Link each bookmarked species to its Species Detail page for further exploration or actions
Allow users to remove species from bookmarks directly from this listing
Serve as the confirmation page after bookmarking tasks such as 'earliest described Muricidae species from the Western Atlantic'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a species name in the bookmarks list

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Bookmarks' header link
Linked from species detail page after bookmarking

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the mollusk species information website, including scope, data sources, and contact information for feedback on species or distribution data.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose and scope of the site, including coverage of mollusk groups, regions, and data types
Provide information on data sources, curation methods, and any acknowledgments or credits
Offer basic contact or feedback information (e.g., how to report data issues)
Link to Help and other key sections for users who want to learn how to use the site

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Help / How to Use' link in About content
Click 'Home' from header/footer while on About page
Click contextual link to educational materials in About content

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'About' footer link
Can be reached directly via about.html deep link

pages.name: Help / How to Use
pages.description: Task-oriented guidance and FAQs for using the site’s search, collections, comparisons, and map tools. Help content mirrors the defined user tasks and links users directly into the relevant tools with preconfigured parameters.
primary_functions: Provide step-by-step guides that mirror common tasks such as advanced species search, building collections, using comparisons, exploring the map, and working with teaching resources
Offer FAQs addressing how to interpret size, depth, conservation status, and trait data on species pages
Explain how user tools work (collections/lists, comparisons, favorites, bookmarks) in a single-user context

Link directly to relevant functional pages (Species Search & Results, Distribution Map, Teaching Resources, Collections, Comparisons, Favorites, Bookmarks) to support quick navigation while following help instructions

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a help link for 'Advanced species search' tutorial
Click a help link for 'Browse by group/family'
Click a help link for 'Search by traits (radula type)'
Click a help link for 'Distribution Map' how-to
Click a help link for 'Teaching Resources' how-to
Click a help link for managing collections
Click a help link for managing comparisons
Click a help link for using favorites
Click a help link for using bookmarks

access_methods.description: Accessible via 'Help' header link
Accessible via 'Help / How to Use' footer link

header_links.description: Homepage with global search and quick access to main tools
Basic species search and results listing
Advanced species filters for habitat, depth, size, region, status, and more
Browse species by higher taxonomic groups and families (e.g., cephalopods, Muricidae)
Search species using trait filters such as radula type and other morphological traits
Interactive map of species distributions by region and country
View and manage species collections and thematic lists
View and manage species comparison lists
How-to guides and FAQs for using the site

footer_links.description: Information about the site, data sources, and purpose
Guidance on searching, collections, comparisons, and other tools
